title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts
description: A design copies old TTL dead-letter retry loops into RabbitMQ 4.3, uses priority as unlimited preemption, leaves consumer timeouts unrelated to handler deadlines, and assumes new broker behavior exists on older clusters. Learners cumulatively produce RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
